pattern: Greedily fixing each pair seat by seat, or counting cycles among partner-pairs, gives the minimum swaps as the number of people minus the number of correctly linked groups.
hints: Process the row two seats at a time and make the first seat's partner sit beside it.
Whenever a couple is already together you spend nothing, otherwise one swap places the missing partner.
Walk pairs left to right: if the partner of the left person is not adjacent, swap whoever is there with the partner and count one; the total of these swaps is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: one swap reunites the middle couple
already paired even though not in numeric order
smallest row reversed is still a couple
full rotation needs n-1 swaps for a single cycle
two independent broken couples need two swaps

stress.description: random valid arrangements of n couples, shuffling 2n unique ids
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(30, max(1, size)))
    row = list(range(2 * n))
    rng.shuffle(row)
    return [row]
stress.note: always a permutation of 0..2n-1 so partners are well defined
